titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Henry Grattan Esmonde to Augustine Birrell, 7 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Esmonde

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Thomas Henry Grattan Esmonde (1862-1935), a landowner and politician. The letter was addressed to the Chief Secretary for Ireland, Augustine Birrell (1850-1933). Birrell was appointed chief secretary in January 1907. In 1916 Birrell resigned as he willingly accepted the political responsibility for the outbreak of the Rising in 1916. In the letter Esmonde states that John Robert O'Connell (1868-1943) is qualified for the position of chief crown solicitor which has been left vacant.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/8

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
date>7.4.16. Dear Mr. Birrell The office for Chief
Crown Solicitor for Ireland
being vacant
I have
pleasure in recommending
Sir John P. O'Connell of
34 Kildare St Dublin
for the post. He is well qualified for
the duties of that office. Ever Yours Thomas H. Grattan Easmond
